skill.name: write-app-change-log
skill.description: Creates a concise Android app changelog from recent git history and updates the latest Fastlane changelog file.
skill.summary: Drafts release changelog text from git commits and existing app release notes.
skill.author: AnySoftKeyboard

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found legitimate git command examples and changelog file edits, with no prompt injection or malicious exfiltration evidence. Publish with a warning because the skill directs repository commands and file modification.

medium_findings.title: Repository Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run git fetch, git tag, and git log commands. These commands are normal for changelog generation, but they still execute repository commands and should be limited to trusted workspaces.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain explicit git command examples. The context is legitimate release documentation, so the concern is operational risk, not confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Repository File Read and Write Instructions
medium_findings.description: The workflow asks the assistant to read existing changelog and build files, then update the latest changelog file. This is expected behavior for the skill, but it can modify repository content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines directly reference reading project files and updating a changelog file. The scope is specific and local, with no evidence of secret access or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detections
low_findings.description: Static analysis marked lines 3, 22, and 46 as weak cryptography. These lines describe changelog generation, commit descriptions, and release note format. No cryptographic algorithm usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain prose and release-note formatting guidance only. I found no hash, cipher, or password storage behavior in those locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive System Reconnaissance Detection
low_findings.description: Static analysis marked line 56 as system reconnaissance. The line only says to avoid technical jargon unless necessary, which is content guidance, not system discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain writing guidance. I found no command, filesystem enumeration, host inspection, or environment probing there.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell command examples for git operations. They are not obfuscated and do not include user-controlled shell interpolation, but users should review commands before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and easy to inspect. They appear necessary for the stated changelog task and show no injection or malware pattern.

content.value_statement: Release changelogs often miss important user-facing changes from commit history. This skill reviews recent git commits and updates the latest Fastlane changelog in the project style.
actual_capabilities: Fetches and identifies the latest version tag pattern.
Reviews commits between the latest tag and HEAD.
Filters commits for user-facing app changes.
Matches the tone of existing Fastlane changelog files.
Drafts concise release notes for Android app updates.
Updates the highest numbered changelog file.
limitations: Requires a git repository with version tags.
Assumes changelogs use the Fastlane Android metadata path.
Needs human review for release accuracy.
Does not publish releases or create version tags.

use_cases.title: Prepare Store Release Notes
use_cases.description: Generate concise text for the latest Android app release before submitting store metadata.

use_cases.title: Summarize App Changes
use_cases.description: Turn recent commits into user-facing release notes that avoid internal implementation details.

use_cases.title: Maintain Changelog Style
use_cases.description: Update changelog files while matching previous tone, structure, and release wording.

prompt_templates.title: Create Latest Changelog
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the write-app-change-log skill to update the latest Android changelog from recent git history.

prompt_templates.title: Review Before Editing
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze recent commits and existing changelogs, then show me the proposed release notes before changing any file.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on User Impact
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a changelog that includes only user-facing features, bug fixes, and performance improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Match Existing Release Style
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the Fastlane changelog history, identify the latest version name and code, and update the newest changelog file.

output_examples.input: Recent commits add keyboard layout fixes and improve startup speed.
output_examples.output: A concise changelog entry that highlights improved startup performance and corrected keyboard layout behavior.

output_examples.input: Recent commits mostly update CI, dependencies, and internal documentation.
output_examples.output: A short release note that omits internal maintenance changes and keeps only meaningful app behavior changes.

output_examples.input: Existing changelogs use one welcome line followed by bullet points.
output_examples.output: A matching release note with the same welcome format and brief user-facing bullet points.

best_practices: Review the proposed changelog before committing it.
Confirm version name and version code from project files.
Keep release notes focused on user-visible changes.
anti_patterns: Do not include CI or build system changes unless users are affected.
Do not copy raw commit messages directly into store notes.
Do not update release files without checking the existing style.

faq.question: What repository format does this skill expect?
faq.answer: It expects an Android project that stores Fastlane changelogs under the standard metadata directory.

faq.question: Does it create release tags?
faq.answer: No. It reads existing tags to decide which commits belong in the changelog.

faq.question: Can it ignore internal maintenance commits?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow excludes CI, infrastructure, and internal documentation changes unless they affect app users.

faq.question: Does it need network access?
faq.answer: It may fetch git tags from the configured remote repository. Review that command before running it.

faq.question: Will it edit files?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow updates the highest numbered Fastlane changelog file.

faq.question: Should the output be reviewed?
faq.answer: Yes. A maintainer should verify version details and user-facing accuracy before release.
